title: Nvidia Q4 FY26 Earnings Call | $NVDA | 🔴 WATCH LIVE
transcript: Heat. Hey, Heat. Heat. Heat. N. Heat. Heat. >> [music] >> Heat. Heat. Happy birthday. Heat. Heat. [music] Heat. Heat. By daddy gy. [music] I'm doo. Heat. Heat. >> [music] [music] >> Hey everybody. Heat. Heat. Little heat. >> [music] >> Heat. Hey, Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. Heat. Good afternoon. My name is Sarah and I will be your conference operator today. At this time, I would like to welcome everyone to Nvidia's fourth quarter earnings call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent any background noise. After the speaker's remarks, there will be a question and answer session. If you would like to ask a question during this time, simply press star followed by the number one on your telephone keypad. If you would like to withdraw your question, press star one again. Thank you. So, you may begin your conference. >> Thank you. Good afternoon everyone and welcome to Nvidia's conference call for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026. With me today from Nvidia are Jensen Wong, president and chief executive officer, and Colette Crest, executive vice president and chief financial officer. Our call is being webcast live on Nvidia's investor relations website. The webcast will be available for replay until the conference call to discuss our financial results for the first quarter of fiscal 2027. The content of today's call is Nvidia's property. It can't be reproduced or transcribed without our prior written consent. During this call, we may make forward-looking statements based on current expectations. These are subject to a number of significant risks and uncertainties, and our actual results may differ materially. For a discussion of factors that could affect our future financial results in business, please refer to the disclosure in today's earnings release, our most recent forms 10K and 10Q, and the reports that we may file on form 8K with the Securities and Exchange Commission. All our statements are m made as as of today, February 25th, 2026 based on informat

key_points: 어젠틱 AI 변곡점 선언 — 2~3개월 전 개막, 코딩·문제해결 실용화 단계 진입
NVLink 72: 이전 세대 대비 에너지효율 토큰 생성 50배 향상
2030년 데이터센터 캐팩스 3~4조 달러 전망 유효 — AI 소프트웨어가 클래식 대비 1,000배 연산 필요
Groq와 저지연 추론 기술 비독점 라이선스 계약 체결
Hopper GPU 우주 배치 완료 — 세계 최초 우주 GPU
Anthropic 매출 1년 10배 성장에도 공급 부족 — NVIDIA 수요 급증 방증
CY2027 성장 가시성 확보 (대규모 구매 약정 반영)
speech_summary.full_summary: NVIDIA Q4 FY26 실적발표 어닝콜. 젠슨 황 CEO는 어젠틱 AI가 2~3개월 전 변곡점을 돌파했다고 선언하며, 코딩·문제해결 분야에서 이미 실용화 단계에 접어들었음을 강조했다. NVLink 72 아키텍처(Blackwell)로 이전 세대 대비 에너지 효율 50배 향상을 달성했으며, AI 소프트웨어가 요구하는 연산량은 클래식 컴퓨팅 대비 1,000배에 달해 2030년 데이터센터 캐팩스 3~4조 달러 전망이 충분히 합리적이라고 주장했다. Anthropic·Meta·OpenAI·xAI 등 주요 AI 기업 전방위 파트너십을 발표했으며, 저지연 추론 기업 Groq와의 비독점 라이선스 계약을 체결해 NVIDIA 아키텍처에 통합 예정이라고 밝혔다. Hopper GPU가 이미 우주에 배치되어 초고해상도 위성 이미징 AI 처리에 활용 중임도 공개했다. CFO 콜레트 크레스는 공급망 생태계 지원을 최우선으로 하면서도 자사주 매입·배당을 지속 중이라고 밝혔다.
speech_summary.market_impact_summary: 어닝콜 종료(23:07 UTC, 08:07 KST) 직후 GOOGL·AMD·MU·AVGO 애프터마켓에서 강한 변동성 포착. 특히 GOOGL(23:14 UTC, 1위)과 AMD(23:15 UTC, 2위)가 어닝콜 종료 직후 즉각 반응. MU는 어닝콜 도중(22:57~23:05 UTC) 이미 반응하며 젠슨 황 AI 발언 구간과 시간적 연동. BTC·ETH도 리스크온 분위기 동반 반응. NQ/ES 선물 데이터는 38일 경과로 수집 불가.

top_volatility.transcript_segment: Vera is off the charts better than that. Your next question comes from Tim Aruri with UBS. Your line is open. Thanks a lot. Um Klet, I was wondering if you can talk about the deployment of um capital. Uh I know that you really jacked up the purchase commits. Uh but it sounds like maybe you're over the hump on this and you're going to probably generate about a hundred million in cash this year. So uh and you know pretty much no matter how good the results have been, the stock hasn't really gone up gone up much. So I would think it's that you probably feel like this is a pretty uh uh good price to be you know buying back a bunch of it here. So I was wondering if you can talk abouthat like you know question being why not put a big stake in the ground and just you know have a huge share repo here. Thanks. So uh thanks for the question. Um we look at our capital return uh very very carefully and we do believe that one of the most important things that we can do is really supporting the extreme oak um ecosystem that's in front of us that stems from everywhere from our suppliers and the work that we need to do to asssure that we can have the supply that's needed and help them from a capacity all the way that we are in terms of the early developers of the AI solution. solutions that will be on our platform. So we will continue to make this a very important part of our process and strategic investments. But of course we are still repurchasing our stock. We are still with our dividend as well and we will continue to find the right unique opportunities within the year uh for doing those different purchases. Your final question comes from Jim Schneider with Goldman Sachs. Your line is open. Thank you for taking my question. Uh Jonathan, you've previously outlined the potential to get to 3 to 4 trillion of data center capex by 2030 which implies a potential acceleration in growth rates which you've sort of uh guided to this at least this next quarter. The question is, what are some of the key application areas that you believe are most likely to drive that inflection? Is that physical AI, agentic or something else? And do you still feel good about thathree to four trillion dollar envelope? Thank you. Yeah, let's let's uh let's back that up and and just reason through it from a a few different ways. So the first way is uh our first principles. The way that software is done in the future using AI is token driven and I think you you everybody talks about tokconomics and talks about data centers generating tokens and inference is about generating tokens and um we generate tokens you know we're just talking about tokens uhow Nvidia's NVLink 72 enabled us to generate tokens at 50 times better performance per unit energy than the previous generation. And so token generation is at the center of almost everything that relates to software in the future and relates to computing. Uh if you look at look at the way we use computing in the past. However, uh the amount of computation demand for software in the past is a a tiny fraction of what is necessary in the future. And AI is here. AI is not going to go back. uh AI is only going only get better from here. And so if you think about it and you said okay well the world was investing about three to 400 billion dollars a year in classical computing and now AI is here and the amount of computation necessary is a thousand times higher than uh the way we used to do computing. the computing demand is just a lot higher. And so if if we continue to believe there's value in it, and we'll talk about that in a second, then then uh the world will invest uh to produce that token. And so the amount of token generation capability uh that the world needs is a lot more than $700 billion. And and uh I'm fairly confidenthat we're going to continue to generate tokens. We're going to continue to invest in compute capacity um from this point out and and uh fundamentally becausevery single company depends on software. Every software will depend on AI and so every company will produce tokens and that's the reason why I call them AI factories and and uh whether your company in the cloud data centers you have AI factories to generate tokens for your revenues. If you're enterprise software company, you're going to generate tokens for the agentic systems that are on top of your tools. Uh if you are a robotics factory
top_volatility.transcript_segment_ko: 【UBS 팀 아루리 Q】 자본 배분 전략 — 구매 약정 급증 대비 자사주 매입 확대 여지 문의. 【콜레트 크레스 CFO】 공급망·생태계 지원이 최우선이나 자사주 매입·배당 지속 중. 【골드만삭스 짐 슈나이더 Q】 2030년 데이터센터 캐팩스 3~4조 달러 목표 유효성 및 핵심 동인 문의. 【젠슨 황】 미래 소프트웨어는 토큰 중심 — NVLink 72로 에너지효율 이전 세대 대비 50배 향상. 클래식 컴퓨팅 대비 AI 연산 수요는 1,000배 높음. 모든 기업이 'AI 팩토리'를 통해 토큰을 생산하는 시대 → 3~4조 달러 전망 유효.

top_volatility.transcript_segment: generate the outcome of this new software called we call AI agentic AI and so the amount of computationecessary um is far far greater than pre-recorded You know, just as just as a a computer has a lot more computation capability than a DVD recorder, a DVD player that was pre-recorded, artificial intelligence needs a lot more computing capability than the way we used to do software in the past. Now the question about the question about um uh computation about sustainability at the first level level is just at the computer science level this is the way computing is going to be done. Now from an industrial level because all of our companies in the final analysis are powered by software and the cloud companies are powered by software and if the new softwarequires tokens to be generated and the tokens are monetized, then it stands to reason that their data center buildout directly drives their revenues. And so compute drives revenues and I think they all understand that. I think people are increasingly starting to understand that as well. And then lastly, um you know, the benefits that AI produces uh for the world ultimately has to generate revenues. And we're seeing right in front right right being developed as we see as as we stand here. Um Agentic AI has turned an inflection point and it literally happened in the last couple of two three months. Of course, inside the industry, we've been seeing it for a while, you know, probably 6 months or so. Um, but the world is now awakened to the agentic AI inflection. The agents are super smart. They're solving real problems. Coding is obviously supported by agentic systems now. And all of our coders here at NVIDIA are using agentic systems either cloud code or open AAI codecs um enormously to uh and oftentimes both and cursor oftentimes all three depends on the use case um but they have agents and co- co you know design partners engineering partners to help them solve problems and you could see their revenue skyrocket you know these companies In the case of Anthropic, I think theirevenues 10xed in a year and they are severely capacity constrained because demand is just incredible and the the token demand is incredible. The token generation rate is growing exponentially and the same thing with of course open AAI their demand is incredible and so the more compute that they can stand online bring online the faster theirevenues will grow. And that goes back
top_volatility.transcript_segment_ko: 【젠슨 황】 어젠틱 AI는 2~3개월 전 변곡점 돌파 — DVD 재생기 vs 컴퓨터처럼 AI 소프트웨어는 기존 방식 대비 훨씬 많은 연산 필요. 클라우드 기업들은 데이터센터 확장이 직접 매출로 연결됨을 인식. NVIDIA 내부도 Claude Code·OpenAI Codex·Cursor 등 어젠틱 시스템 풀 활용 중. Anthropic 매출 1년 만에 10배 성장에도 공급이 수요를 못 따라가는 상황 — 토큰 수요 기하급수적 증가 중.

top_volatility.transcript_segment: Um, and what do the economics look like today and how do you think that could evolve over time? Thank you. Well, the economics are poor today, but it's going to improve over time. Uh, as you know, as you know, the way that space works is radically different than how it works down here. There's an abundance of energy. Um, uh, but solar panels are large, but there's plenty of space in space. Uh, the en the heat dissipation, it's cold in space. Um, however, uh, there's no there's no air flow. And so the only way to to uh dissipate heat is through conduction and the radiators that you need to create are fairly large. Um liquid cooling is obviously out of the question because it's kind of it's heavy and you know freezes and and so the methods that we use here on Earth are a little different than the way we would do it in space. Um but there are many different computing problems that really wants to be done in space. And so uh Nvidia is is already the world's first GPU in space. Hopper is in space. And um uh one of the best use cases of uh GPUs in space is imaging to be able to imaging image at extremely high resolutions using using of course of course uh optics and and and artificial intelligence and to be able to do that computation of reprojection of different angles and be able to to upres and ando noise reduction and and just be able to see be able to image at very large, very high resolutions, extremely large scales and very, very fast. It's hard to do that um by sending, you know, pabytes and pedabytes of imaging data back here on Earth and doing that work. It's easier just to do it out in space and then and then um ignore ignore all of the data collected and processed until you see something interesting. And so artificial intelligence in space will
top_volatility.transcript_segment_ko: 【Q】 우주 AI 컴퓨팅의 현재 경제성과 미래 전망. 【젠슨 황】 현재 경제성은 낮지만 개선 예정. 우주는 에너지·공간 풍부하나 방열이 핵심 과제(공기 없어 전도·방열판만 가능, 액체 냉각 불가). NVIDIA는 이미 세계 최초 GPU를 우주에 올림 — Hopper GPU 우주 배치 완료. 핵심 활용: 초고해상도 위성 이미징 + AI 처리. 페타바이트급 이미징 데이터를 지구로 전송하는 대신 우주에서 직접 AI 처리하여 효율 극대화.

We're uniquely positioned to partner with frontier model builders at every stage training inference and AI factory scaleout. Finally, we recently entered into a non-exclusive licensing agreement with Grock for its low latency inference technology and welcomed a team of brilliant engineers to Nvidia. As we did with Melanox, we will extend NVIDIA's architecture with Gro's innovations to enable new levels of AI infrastructure performance and value. We look forward to sharing more at GTC next month. Okay, back to you. We will now transition to Q&A. Operator, please pull for questions.
